{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Receptionist",
  "description": "Atutech is a software and technology services company primarily focused on delivering digital solutions and tech support for businesses. It is registered as a limited company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description

We are looking for a Receptionist to manage our front desk on a daily basis and&nbsp;


	administrative and clerical tasks.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome guests as soon as they arrive at the office
	Direct visitors to the appropriate person and office
	Answer, screen and forward incoming phone calls
	Ensure reception area is tidy and presentable, with all necessary stationery and material (e.g. pens, forms and brochures)
	Provide basic and accurate information in-person and via phone/email
	Receive, sort and distribute daily mail/deliveries
	Maintain office security by following safety procedures and controlling access via the reception desk (monitor logbook, issue visitor badges)
	Order front office supplies and keep inventory of stock
	Update calendars and schedule meetings
	Arrange travel and accommodations, and prepare vouchers
	Keep updated records of office expenses and costs
	Perform other clerical receptionist duties such as filing, photocopying, transcribing and faxing


Requirements


	Work ideas as a Receptionist, Front Office Representative or similar role
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Hands-on experience with office equipment (e.g. fax machines and printers)
	Professional attitude and appearance
	Solid written and verbal communication skills
	Ability to be resourceful and proactive when issues arise
	Excellent organizational skills
	Multitasking and time-management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;atutechscl@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of the mail.",

Receptionist Job at Atutechs C Limited




        
    

        
        Atutech is a software and technology services company primarily focused on delivering digital solutions and tech support for businesses. It is registered as a limited company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description

We are looking for a Receptionist to manage our front desk on a daily basis and 


	administrative and clerical tasks.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Greet and welcome guests as soon as they arrive at the office
	Direct visitors to the appropriate person and office
	Answer, screen and forward incoming phone calls
	Ensure reception area is tidy and presentable, with all necessary stationery and material (e.g. pens, forms and brochures)
	Provide basic and accurate information in-person and via phone/email
	Receive, sort and distribute daily mail/deliveries
	Maintain office security by following safety procedures and controlling access via the reception desk (monitor logbook, issue visitor badges)
	Order front office supplies and keep inventory of stock
	Update calendars and schedule meetings
	Arrange travel and accommodations, and prepare vouchers
	Keep updated records of office expenses and costs
	Perform other clerical receptionist duties such as filing, photocopying, transcribing and faxing


Requirements


	Work ideas as a Receptionist, Front Office Representative or similar role
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Hands-on experience with office equipment (e.g. fax machines and printers)
	Professional attitude and appearance
	Solid written and verbal communication skills
	Ability to be resourceful and proactive when issues arise
	Excellent organizational skills
	Multitasking and time-management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.

title: Supermarket Manager Job at Kiyix Recruitment Hub
content:
At Kiyix Recruitment Hub, we help make recruitment a stress-free experience for both the employer and the potential job seekers Our goal at Kiyix Recruitment and Motivation Hub is to help create one million jobs in Nigeria by 2030. We strive to help make businesses in Africa great by providing them with good staff and enabling the staff to become great.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supermarket Manager

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Supermarket Manager role involves overseeing daily store operations, maintaining cleanliness, order, and safety standards, managing staff, implementing sales strategies, ensuring exceptional customer service, and maintaining inventory control.
	The manager will be tasked with ensuring proper product display, availability, and merchandising, ensure store’s efficiency, meeting sales targets, managing budgets, monitoring customer experience and resolving issues promptly. This is a full-time, on-site position located in Apapa, Lagos.


Qualifications


	A bachelor’s degree/OND / HND in Business Administration, Retail Management, or a related field is an advantage.
	2–4 years of experiencein Supermarket or retail store management with a deep understanding of daily operations.
	Strong Customer Service and Communication skills with the ability to address customer needs effectively and lead a team.
	Proficiency in Sales strategies and the ability to meet or exceed sales targets.
	Knowledge of Inventory Control practices, including stock management and ordering processes.
	Excellent leadership, problem-solving, and organisational skills.
	Proficiency in basic IT tools and retail software is a plus.
	Previous experience in managing teams and budgets is beneficial.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Supermarket - Manager" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Assistant to the MD",
  "description": "Ezekiel Properties Management Company&nbsp; is a multifaceted real estate and property solutions company, specializing in luxury property development, mortgage advisory, joint ventures, construction, and agro-estate projects. We combine industry expertise, strategic partnerships, and innovative practices to deliver high-value, sustainable, and profitable real estate solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the MD

Job Location:&nbsp;Maitama, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

About&nbsp;the&nbsp;Role


	This role provides high-level executive and operational support to senior leadership within a fast-paced real estate and business development environment.
	The Executive Assistant will function as a strategic coordination point&mdash;ensuring that priorities are translated into execution, communication is streamlined, and leadership bandwidth is optimized for decision-making and growth.
	The position requires a high degree of discretion, structure, and situational awareness, with the ability to manage competing priorities across administrative, operational, and field-related activities.
	In addition to office-based responsibilities in Maitama, Abuja, the role includes up to 25% travel to the Eastern region of Nigeria to support project coordination, stakeholder engagement, and on-ground execution.


Core&nbsp;Responsibilities


	Executive Support &amp; Calendar Management
	Manage complex calendars, scheduling meetings, site visits, and strategic sessions across multiple stakeholders.
	Coordinate travel itineraries, accommodation, and logistics for leadership, ensuring efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
	Prepare briefing materials, reports, and documentation ahead of meetings and engagements.
	Track deliverables and follow up on action points to ensure timely execution
	Act as a liaison between leadership and internal teams, ensuring alignment on priorities and timelines.
	Support the execution of business initiatives by coordinating across departments (operations, sales, admin).
	Monitor workflow systems and escalate bottlenecks proactively.
	Maintain organized documentation and filing systems for operational continuity.
	Communication &amp; Stakeholder Management
	Draft and manage professional correspondence on behalf of leadership.
	Interface with clients, partners, and government or regulatory stakeholders when required.
	Ensure consistency and professionalism in all outward-facing communication.
	Handle sensitive information with a high level of confidentiality and judgment.
	Travel &amp; Field Support (Eastern Nigeria &ndash; Up to 25%)
	Coordinate logistics, site schedules, and stakeholder meetings during travel.
	Document field observations, project updates, and action items for leadership review.
	Support relationship management with regional stakeholders and vendors.
	Research, Reporting &amp; Decision Support
	Conduct targeted research to support business decisions (market insights, property intelligence, vendor analysis).
	Compile reports, dashboards, and summaries to support executive decision-making.
	Track key operational and strategic metrics where required.
	Key Relationships:
	Senior Leadership / Principal
	Operations and Admin Teams
	Sales and Business Development Teams
	External Stakeholders (Clients, Partners, Vendors, Government Agencies)


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Communication, Social Sciences, or related field


Experience:


	2&ndash;5 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Administrative, or Operations Coordination role
	Experience in real estate, consulting, or structured corporate environments is an advantage


Knowledge:


	Strong understanding of administrative systems and executive support structures
	Basic knowledge of business operations, project coordination, and stakeholder management
	Familiarity with Google Drive workspace
	Familiarity with travel coordination and logistics within Nigeria


Skills:


	Strong organizational and time management capability
	Clear and professional written and verbal communication
	High attention to detail and execution discipline
	Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities under pressure
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace tools


Behavioral Competencies:


	High level of discretion and confidentiality
	Proactive and solution-oriented mindset
	Strong sense of ownership and accountability
	Adaptability and willingness to travel as required
	Professional presence and interpersonal effectiveness


Additional Requirements:


	Must be based in Abuja (proximity to Maitama axis preferred)
	Willingness and availability to travel up to 25% to Eastern Nigeria
	Ability to work in a dynamic, fast-evolving business environment
	Strong alignment with performance-driven and execution-focused culture

Executive Assistant to the MD Job at Ezekiel Properties Management Company




        
    

        
        Ezekiel Properties Management Company  is a multifaceted real estate and property solutions company, specializing in luxury property development, mortgage advisory, joint ventures, construction, and agro-estate projects. We combine industry expertise, strategic partnerships, and innovative practices to deliver high-value, sustainable, and profitable real estate solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the MD

Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This role provides high-level executive and operational support to senior leadership within a fast-paced real estate and business development environment.
	The Executive Assistant will function as a strategic coordination point—ensuring that priorities are translated into execution, communication is streamlined, and leadership bandwidth is optimized for decision-making and growth.
	The position requires a high degree of discretion, structure, and situational awareness, with the ability to manage competing priorities across administrative, operational, and field-related activities.
	In addition to office-based responsibilities in Maitama, Abuja, the role includes up to 25% travel to the Eastern region of Nigeria to support project coordination, stakeholder engagement, and on-ground execution.


Core Responsibilities


	Executive Support & Calendar Management
	Manage complex calendars, scheduling meetings, site visits, and strategic sessions across multiple stakeholders.
	Coordinate travel itineraries, accommodation, and logistics for leadership, ensuring efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
	Prepare briefing materials, reports, and documentation ahead of meetings and engagements.
	Track deliverables and follow up on action points to ensure timely execution
	Act as a liaison between leadership and internal teams, ensuring alignment on priorities and timelines.
	Support the execution of business initiatives by coordinating across departments (operations, sales, admin).
	Monitor workflow systems and escalate bottlenecks proactively.
	Maintain organized documentation and filing systems for operational continuity.
	Communication & Stakeholder Management
	Draft and manage professional correspondence on behalf of leadership.
	Interface with clients, partners, and government or regulatory stakeholders when required.
	Ensure consistency and professionalism in all outward-facing communication.
	Handle sensitive information with a high level of confidentiality and judgment.
	Travel & Field Support (Eastern Nigeria – Up to 25%)
	Coordinate logistics, site schedules, and stakeholder meetings during travel.
	Document field observations, project updates, and action items for leadership review.
	Support relationship management with regional stakeholders and vendors.
	Research, Reporting & Decision Support
	Conduct targeted research to support business decisions (market insights, property intelligence, vendor analysis).
	Compile reports, dashboards, and summaries to support executive decision-making.
	Track key operational and strategic metrics where required.
	Key Relationships:
	Senior Leadership / Principal
	Operations and Admin Teams
	Sales and Business Development Teams
	External Stakeholders (Clients, Partners, Vendors, Government Agencies)


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Communication, Social Sciences, or related field


Experience:


	2–5 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Administrative, or Operations Coordination role
	Experience in real estate, consulting, or structured corporate environments is an advantage


Knowledge:


	Strong understanding of administrative systems and executive support structures
	Basic knowledge of business operations, project coordination, and stakeholder management
	Familiarity with Google Drive workspace
	Familiarity with travel coordination and logistics within Nigeria


Skills:


	Strong organizational and time management capability
	Clear and professional written and verbal communication
	High attention to detail and execution discipline
	Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities under pressure
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace tools


Behavioral Competencies:


	High level of discretion and confidentiality
	Proactive and solution-oriented mindset
	Strong sense of ownership and accountability
	Adaptability and willingness to travel as required
	Professional presence and interpersonal effectiveness


Additional Requirements:


	Must be based in Abuja (proximity to Maitama axis preferred)
	Willingness and availability to travel up to 25% to Eastern Nigeria
	Ability to work in a dynamic, fast-evolving business environment
	Strong alignment with performance-driven and execution-focused culture


     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Civil Engineer",
  "description": "Metal, Clay and Potter Limited is a construction-based subsidiary of Buckler Systems Limited, specialized in the design and development of standard structures for armoured equipment in supporting defense infrastructure and specialized engineering projects.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Epe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview


	The Civil Engineer at Metal, Clay and Potter Limited is responsible for planning, designing, and overseeing the construction and maintenance of infrastructure projects, ensuring they are safe, cost-effective, and completed in compliance with engineering standards and regulations.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop comprehensive project plans that outline scope, resources, and timelines.
	Utilize CAD software and other design tools to model and simulate project designs.
	Supervise on-site construction activities to ensure adherence to project specifications and safety standards.
	Conduct regular site inspections to ensure quality control and safety compliance.
	Implement and oversee quality control measures to ensure construction materials and workmanship meet specified standards.
	Assess and manage risks associated with construction projects from planning to execution
	Ensure that all documentations comply with regulatory and organizational standards


Qualifications and Skills


	Educational Background: HND or Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering or related roles
	Experience: Minimum of 2 - 4 years of experience in Construction and Project Management.
	Certification: Memberships in professional bodies (e.g., NSE, COREN) is an added advantage.
	Operational &amp; Technical Skills: Strong knowledge and proficiency in industry-specific software, like AutoCAD Civil 3D, and other computer-aided design software.
	Personal Attributes / Professionalism: Ability to work with independently with minimal supervision.


Why Work With Us?


	Attractive &amp; competitive salary package
	13th month bonus
	Generous annual leave to recharge
	Continuous learning &amp; development opportunities
	Clear career growth &amp; advancement path
	Supportive and inclusive workplace culture
	Be part of a company that values innovation &amp; excellence
	Comprehensive health &amp; wellness benefits
	Make a real difference working on projects that protect lives, strengthen security, and keep communities safe.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to:&nbsp;hr@bucklersystems.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Civil Engineer Job at Metal, Clay and Potter Limited




        
    

        
        Metal, Clay and Potter Limited is a construction-based subsidiary of Buckler Systems Limited, specialized in the design and development of standard structures for armoured equipment in supporting defense infrastructure and specialized engineering projects.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location: Epe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Civil Engineer at Metal, Clay and Potter Limited is responsible for planning, designing, and overseeing the construction and maintenance of infrastructure projects, ensuring they are safe, cost-effective, and completed in compliance with engineering standards and regulations.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop comprehensive project plans that outline scope, resources, and timelines.
	Utilize CAD software and other design tools to model and simulate project designs.
	Supervise on-site construction activities to ensure adherence to project specifications and safety standards.
	Conduct regular site inspections to ensure quality control and safety compliance.
	Implement and oversee quality control measures to ensure construction materials and workmanship meet specified standards.
	Assess and manage risks associated with construction projects from planning to execution
	Ensure that all documentations comply with regulatory and organizational standards


Qualifications and Skills


	Educational Background: HND or Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or related roles
	Experience: Minimum of 2 - 4 years of experience in Construction and Project Management.
	Certification: Memberships in professional bodies (e.g., NSE, COREN) is an added advantage.
	Operational & Technical Skills: Strong knowledge and proficiency in industry-specific software, like AutoCAD Civil 3D, and other computer-aided design software.
	Personal Attributes / Professionalism: Ability to work with independently with minimal supervision.


Why Work With Us?


	Attractive & competitive salary package
	13th month bonus
	Generous annual leave to recharge
	Continuous learning & development opportunities
	Clear career growth & advancement path
	Supportive and inclusive workplace culture
	Be part of a company that values innovation & excellence
	Comprehensive health & wellness benefits
	Make a real difference working on projects that protect lives, strengthen security, and keep communities safe.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Registered Nurse",
  "description": "LEAM Consulting Limited is the foremost consulting firm that stands out in areas of professional certification training, HR advisory services, Business Development / Startup, and IT consulting services in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Registered Nurse

Job Location:&nbsp;Iwo Road, Egbeda, Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We&#39;re looking for a compassionate and skilled Registered Nurse to join our healthcare team.
	You&#39;ll provide high-quality patient care, support nursing teams, and contribute to improving patient outcomes.


Responsibilities


	Supervise nursing operations and patient care delivery
	Support and mentor nursing staff to maintain high standards.
	Deliver patient-centered care in various clinical setting
	Assess, plan, implement, and evaluate patient care
	Support junior staff and students
	Maintain accurate patient records


Requirements / Qualifications


	B.Sc. Nursing / RN (Registered Nurse) certification.
	Minimum 2 years of clinical experience
	Current license with Nursing &amp; Midwifery Council of Nigeria (NMCN).
	Strong clinical skills and communication.


Salary
N120,000 - N180,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruiting@leamconsulting.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-16",

Registered Nurse Job at LEAM Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        LEAM Consulting Limited is the foremost consulting firm that stands out in areas of professional certification training, HR advisory services, Business Development / Startup, and IT consulting services in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Registered Nurse

Job Location: Iwo Road, Egbeda, Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We're looking for a compassionate and skilled Registered Nurse to join our healthcare team.
	You'll provide high-quality patient care, support nursing teams, and contribute to improving patient outcomes.


Responsibilities


	Supervise nursing operations and patient care delivery
	Support and mentor nursing staff to maintain high standards.
	Deliver patient-centered care in various clinical setting
	Assess, plan, implement, and evaluate patient care
	Support junior staff and students
	Maintain accurate patient records


Requirements / Qualifications


	B.Sc. Nursing / RN (Registered Nurse) certification.
	Minimum 2 years of clinical experience
	Current license with Nursing & Midwifery Council of Nigeria (NMCN).
	Strong clinical skills and communication.


Salary
N120,000 - N180,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
